parts.text: A config is advice. A hook is a wall. mergegate installs the gate into git itself.
Block the push — not just the pull request. Right where the agent acts.

The gate lives in git's pre-push hook. An agent pushes its change straight at main — and the push is rejected on the spot, before it ever reaches the remote. The agent fixes it properly, references the spec, and the very same push lands clean.
mergegate. The line nothing crosses unproven. From deemwar.
